records.ety: [F. entretenir; entre between (L. inter) + tenir to hold, L. tenere. See Tenable.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Entertained (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Entertaining.]

defs: To be at the charges of; to take or keep in one's service; to maintain; to support; to harbor; to keep.
quotes: You, sir, I entertain for one of my hundred.

defs: To give hospitable reception and maintenance to; to receive at one's board, or into one's house; to receive as a guest.
quotes: Be not forgetful to entertain strangers; for thereby some have entertained unawares.

defs: To engage the attention of agreeably; to amuse with that which makes the time pass pleasantly; to divert; as, to entertain friends with conversation, etc.
quotes: The weary time she can not entertain.

defs: To give reception to; to receive, in general; to receive and take into consideration; to admit, treat, or make use of; as, to entertain a proposal.
quotes: I am not here going to entertain so large a theme as the philosophy of Locke.
A rumor gained ground, -- and, however absurd, was entertained by some very sensible people.

defs: To meet or encounter, as an enemy.
To keep, hold, or maintain in the mind with favor; to keep in the mind; to harbor; to cherish; as, to entertain sentiments.
To lead on; to bring along; to introduce.
quotes: To baptize all nations, and entertain them into the services institutions of the holy Jesus.

defs: To receive, or provide entertainment for, guests; as, he entertains generously.
